title: The God who Keeps us Everyday
summary: “I the LORD do keep it; I will water it every moment: lest any hurt it, I will keep it night and day.” Isaiah 27:3 The holidays are soon over, and we will all begin a new season at school, work etc. Some of you may be excited at the prospect of a new challenge – new friends, teachers, places and colleagues, for example, while others may feel some tension at the thought of new changes. Thankfully, Jesus is the God of the everyday. Speaking about Israel, which also extends to the Church, God says in Isaiah 27:3, “I the LORD do keep it; I will water it every moment: lest any hurt it, I will keep it night and day”. I want to comfort you with this promise of God today: God will keep you first. To keep something is to support it and ensure it endures despite the challenges that may lie ahead. Children and adolescents, God will give you new friends and good, kind teachers. He will further keep you from bad habits and bless your academic skills and results. Young people please say Amen! To those who are employed in diverse activities or are full-time stay-at-home parents, may the Lord Jesus keep you in your job. May you find a deep satisfaction in your labor, may you find favor with your employer and your colleagues!
